YLLgmethods 0.0.0.9000

    • Getting started with YLLgmethods
    • Generalized interventional effects with YLLgmethods

Synthetic survival dataset for YLL examples

Source: R/data.R
yll_toy.Rd

A simulated cohort used to demonstrate the package functions. Each row is one individual followed for up to 15 years, with a binary smoking exposure, a binary hypertension covariate, and a continuous-time-to-event outcome. The data-generating process is encoded in data-raw/yll_toy.R.

Usage

yll_toy

Format

A data frame with 5,000 rows and 9 variables:

id

Integer subject identifier (1..n).

period

Numeric follow-up time, in years, from study entry to event or administrative censoring at year 15.

event

Integer event indicator: 1 if death, 0 if censored.

smoke_binary

Factor exposure with levels "Never" and "Current/Ever".

hypertension

Factor confounder with levels "No" and "Yes".

sex

Factor with levels "Female" and "Male".

education_years

Integer years of completed education (9-20).

bmi

Numeric body-mass index at entry (16.0-40.0).

age

Integer age in years at study entry (35-69).

Source

Simulated. See data-raw/yll_toy.R for the data-generating script.
